02  /  What Testing Misses
Cantena
YMCA renovation. 4 probes. All passed. Flooring failed.
The industry-accepted test: drill, wait 24–72 hrs per probe. 4 probes on 10,000 sqft.

Cantena

A1  /  Market Size
Cantena
$1.3B TAM: Bottom-up build
$83M beachhead → $811M near-term TAM → $1.1–1.3B total TAM
How we get to 3,200 firms
All US commercial construction firms65,660
Mid-size regional firms10,500
Primarily new ground-up construction4,700
With a dedicated super or QCM on staff3,200
How we get to $26K per firm / yr
Moisture probes$2,080
Crew labor$750
Outside lab verification$750
Kits, surface meters, legacy tests$170
What one job costs~$3,750
Typical firm runs 7 jobs a year$26,250
How we get to $1.1–1.3B Starting wedge Full market
United States$83M$453M
Other aligned markets$65M$358M
Near-term TAM$148M$811M
EU + EFTA$88M$400M
Total TAM$236M$1.1–1.3B
Mid-size = $25M–$500M annual revenue. Ground-up = >50% from new builds, not renovation. Below ~$30M, the owner typically covers the QCM role.
Bottom-up from published prices. 52 probes per job. Outside-lab verification on ~1 in 4 jobs. Conservative probe-replacement parity. No value-capture uplift.
Wedge = GC beachhead only. Full market = all 8 construction segments (5.5× multiplier). Other aligned markets: Canada, UK, Ireland, Australia, NZ. EU bloc harmonizing via DIN EN 17668:2022.

Cantena

Platform recurs. Usage scales with projects. Modules expand the firm over time.

Software

Platform floor
$8K / yr
Per firm. Unlimited users, scans, archive, integrations.

Drying prediction, anomaly detection, test readiness.

Insurance data, supplier intel, portfolio view. Ships Y2+.

Role

A3  /  Competitive Analysis
Cantena
4 plays by competitor type
Every competitor is a point-reading instrument; Cantena is the intelligence platform.
Coexist
Wagner
Industry standard probes
They own today’s certified test. Cantena adds intelligence on top.
Compete
Tramex
Surface dielectric meters
Closest physics, same workflow. Cantena wins on depth, spatial, and software.
Supplement
GANN
Chemical method (DACH)
German regulations block replacement. Cantena sits on top as a monitoring layer.
Channel
3rd-party labs
IFTI, NDTI, regional firms
Reseller margins convert cannibalization into distribution.
B1  /  Forward Model
Cantena
How we predict reflectance from moisture
Broadband RF reflection spectrum, layer by layer.
Profile
Physics Pipeline
Water permittivity
Debye relaxation + conductive loss, 1–10 GHz
↓
Composite permittivity
CRIM mixing: water + air + solid, per layer
↓
Wave propagation
Transfer-matrix cascade across N layers
↓
Surface reflection
Γ(f) from cumulative input impedance
Reflection Spectrum
|Γ(f)|
Magnitude vs frequency. What the VNA measures.
Interference features across the sweep encode the moisture gradient.
Every stage uses well-validated physics. The combination, and the inversion behind it, are ours.
B2  /  Bayesian Inversion
Cantena
Recover the profile and know how sure we are
Physics-constrained inversion. Quantified uncertainty at every depth.
Measurement
|Γ(f)| measured
Broadband sweep from the VNA
The only thing the device sees.
Inversion
Propose
Candidate profile + concrete properties
↓
Forward model
Predict the spectrum
↓
Compare
Likelihood vs measurement
↓
Sample
Posterior sample
↺ Repeat until the posterior converges
Posterior
Recovered RH by depth
Posterior mean + 95% credible interval
Every depth comes with its own uncertainty.
We recover the profile. We quantify the uncertainty at every depth.
